whatThisIs: Browse the UB-04 (CMS-1450) revenue-code catalog - the four-digit codes that identify the type of accommodation or ancillary service on an institutional claim. Revenue codes are organized into series: 010x-021x for room and board, 030x for laboratory, 045x for emergency room, 070x for cast room, 0636 for self-administered drugs, 0762 for observation room, and so on. Sophie Well lists every active code with the short description NUBC publishes for it.
whenToUse: Use this when reviewing a hospital UB-04 to understand what each line represents, when a patient asks why their bill shows a 0250 line for pharmacy alongside individual drug HCPCS charges, or when a coder needs to confirm the revenue-code-to-CPT crosswalk for an outpatient procedure. Revenue codes pair with HCPCS on most outpatient lines but stand alone on accommodation lines, so understanding the catalog is the first step in reading any hospital bill or comparing it against an EOB.
